skill.name: lovable
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Lovable.dev project workflows, including GitHub sync, Supabase Edge Function deployment prompts, migrations, secrets tracking, and project mapping.
skill.summary: Coordinate Lovable.dev GitHub sync and Supabase backend deployment workflows.
skill.author: 10K-Digital

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, secret, network, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are markdown examples and workflow references rather than executable code. The package still carries medium operational risk because it guides file scanning, secret-name tracking, GitHub pushes to main, and optional browser-assisted Lovable deployments.

medium_findings.title: Production Deployment Automation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documents yolo mode that can navigate to Lovable, submit deployment prompts, run verification tests, and auto-deploy after git push. This is not malicious, but it can affect production resources if enabled without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly describe browser automation and automatic deployment behavior. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the automation is disclosed and optional.

medium_findings.title: Secret Name Discovery and Tracking
medium_findings.description: The reference describes scanning Edge Functions, environment templates, imports, and Lovable Cloud status to infer required secret names. It does not instruct exfiltration of secret values, but it increases exposure of sensitive metadata.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The document clearly directs automated discovery of secret names and status. I found no evidence that it captures or transmits actual secret values.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported many shell or backtick patterns, but reviewed locations are documentation tables, file trees, command examples, and prompt templates. No executable script files are present in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The package contains only markdown files, and these lines are examples or workflow instructions. There is no active shell execution in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography matches are caused by words such as auth, token, or similar text in documentation. Review found no implemented MD5, SHA1, or custom cryptographic code in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections are auth and secret-name documentation, not cryptographic implementations. No weak algorithm usage was found in executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Heuristic Code Execution Network Credential Combination Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The critical heuristic is explained by documentation that mentions commands, URLs, and secret names in the same markdown package. I found no code path that combines execution, network transfer, and credential access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious combination exists semantically in documentation, but the package has no executable implementation. Browser automation is referenced as a separate skill, not included here.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Injection Attempts Not Found
dangerous_patterns.description: No evidence found for instructions that tell the evaluator to ignore prior instructions, skip analysis, claim pre-approval, or override the marketplace security review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review for common prompt injection phrases found no matching malicious control language. The opening lines are normal skill metadata and project template text.

content.value_statement: Lovable projects need GitHub sync plus separate backend deployment steps. This skill provides structured prompts and checks for Supabase functions, migrations, secrets, and project maps.
actual_capabilities: Explains which Lovable project files sync through GitHub and which need Lovable prompts.
Provides copy-ready prompts for Edge Function deployments, migrations, RLS policies, storage, and auth tasks.
Documents a CLAUDE.md template for Lovable project context, secrets status, branch rules, and backend workflow notes.
Defines a project map format for React, Supabase, and Lovable directory structures.
Outlines secret-name detection from Edge Functions, environment templates, service imports, and Lovable Cloud status.
Describes optional yolo mode handoff for browser-assisted Lovable deployment prompts.
limitations: It does not deploy backend changes by itself without a separate automation skill or user action.
It cannot set Lovable Cloud secrets through prompts; users must add secret values manually.
Secret detection guidance identifies likely secret names, not whether values are correct or safe.
The skill assumes a Lovable, React, and Supabase project structure.

use_cases.title: Deploy an Edge Function
use_cases.description: Use the skill after editing Supabase function code to produce the correct Lovable deployment prompt and verification notes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Database Changes
use_cases.description: Use the migration and RLS prompt references to coordinate schema changes that Lovable must apply outside GitHub sync.

use_cases.title: Document a Lovable Project
use_cases.description: Use the CLAUDE.md template and project map guidance to record structure, backend type, secrets, and sync rules.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Lovable Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project for Lovable.dev workflow needs. Tell me what syncs through GitHub and what needs a Lovable prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Create Deployment Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: I changed the [function name] Supabase Edge Function. Generate the exact Lovable prompt and list any secrets or verification checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Database Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add [database change] in a Lovable project. Create the migration guidance, Lovable prompt, and RLS checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Backend Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Lovable project before release. Check pending functions, migrations, secret names, GitHub sync status, and deployment risks.

output_examples.input: I updated the send-email Edge Function.
output_examples.output: The response names the Lovable deployment prompt, notes required Resend secret setup, and recommends checking function logs after deployment.

output_examples.input: I added a profiles migration with user ownership rules.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes the migration, gives an apply-migrations Lovable prompt, and highlights RLS policies to verify.

output_examples.input: Initialize context for my Lovable project.
output_examples.output: The response creates a concise project context outline with backend type, sync rules, key files, secrets table, and branch guidance.

best_practices: Confirm the project is on the main branch before relying on Lovable GitHub sync.
Review generated Lovable prompts before running destructive database, storage, or auth changes.
Track only secret names and setup status in project documentation, not secret values.
anti_patterns: Do not assume pushing migration files applies them in Lovable Cloud.
Do not store API keys, tokens, or service role keys in CLAUDE.md or repository files.
Do not enable automated deployment mode for production without human review and rollback planning.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy Lovable projects automatically?
faq.answer: No. It primarily provides workflow guidance and Lovable prompts. Automation requires a separate yolo mode skill.

faq.question: Why are Lovable prompts needed after GitHub sync?
faq.answer: Lovable syncs code from the main branch, but backend operations such as migrations and Edge Function deployment need Lovable actions.

faq.question: Can this skill add secrets to Lovable Cloud?
faq.answer: No. It says secrets must be added manually through the Lovable Cloud Secrets interface.

faq.question: What project types does it support best?
faq.answer: It fits Lovable.dev projects that use React, GitHub sync, Supabase Edge Functions, migrations, and Lovable Cloud.

faq.question: Is yolo mode included in this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill references yolo mode and explains when another automation skill may take over.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing backend changes?
faq.answer: Review pending migrations, RLS policies, function secrets, branch status, and whether the Lovable deployment prompt is destructive.
